title: mentha longifolia var. typhoides
intro: Mentha longifolia var. typhoides, also known as Horse Mint or Wild Mint, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and carminative effects, making it useful for treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia var. typhoides include reducing symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and other menstrual-related issues. The plant can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to create salves and topical creams for skin conditions and wounds.
